# CANoe CAPL Test Scripts — Auto-Generated
## ECU: ARDEP_IOController  v1.0.0

Generated by `tools/testgen.py --capl` from `diagnostics_config.yaml`.
**Do not edit manually** — regenerate when the YAML changes.

| `test_did_XXXX.can` | Per-DID exhaustive tests (35 files) |
| `test_dtcs.can` | DTC service tests (0x14 / 0x19) |

## Importing into a CANoe Workspace

### Step 1 — Create Test Setup
```
File → New → Test Setup
```

### Step 2 — Add CAPL Test Module
```
Right-click Test Setup → Insert CAPL Test Module
Select: ecu_diagnostics_test_suite.can
```

### Step 3 — Add per-DID modules
```
Right-click Test Setup → Insert CAPL Test Module
Add each test_did_XXXX.can file
Add test_dtcs.can
```

### Step 4 — Configure CAN database
```
Assign the ECU CAN DB (.dbc) to the test setup
Verify CAN channel assignment matches your hardware
```

### Step 5 — Run
```
Run → Start Test Run
Or: F9 (Run All Tests)
```

---

## CAN Addressing

| Direction | CAN ID |
|-----------|--------|
| Tester → ECU (physical request) | 0x7DF |
| ECU → Tester (response) | 0x7E8 |
| Extended addressing | No (11-bit) |

| P2server_max | P2Server_max | 25 ms |
| P2*server_max | P2*Server_max | 5000 ms |
| S3server | S3Server | 5000 ms |

## Security Algorithm

The generated CAPL scripts use a **key derivation stub** that must be replaced
for production ECUs using `aes128_cmac`.

### Option A — AES-128-CMAC via CAPL DLL
```c
/* In ecu_diagnostics_test_suite.can, replace the key derivation stub: */
key[0..3] = CaplDll_AesCmac128(kSecKeyLevel1, seed, seedLen);
```

### Option B — CANoe .NET / Python node
Delegate key computation to a CANoe scripting node that calls your HSM.

### Development / CI (xor_stub)
If `algorithm: xor_stub` is set in the YAML, the generated XOR stub is correct
as-is. This matches the `bridge.py` simulator and `uds_security_algo.c` stub.

## DTC Catalogue

| Code | Description | Severity |
|------|-------------|----------|
| 0xC00100 | CAN bus communication loss — no frames received > 500ms | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC00200 | CAN bus error passive — TX/RX error counters exceeded 127 | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC00300 | CAN bus off — transmitter disabled by bus-off recovery | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC01000 | LIN bus no response — no slave response within timeout | maintenance_only |
| 0xC01100 | LIN bus framing error — break field or sync byte invalid | maintenance_only |
| 0xB00100 | Output 1 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB00200 | Output 2 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB00300 | Output 3 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB00400 | Output 4 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB00500 | Output 5 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB00600 | Output 6 overcurrent — load current exceeded threshold | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xB01000 | PowerIO open load — output commanded ON but no current detected | maintenance_only |
| 0xC0FF00 | Supply voltage low — below 9.0V for > 500ms | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC0FF10 | Supply voltage high — above 16.0V for > 100ms | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC0FF20 | ECU overtemperature — internal junction > 125°C | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC0FF30 | Watchdog reset event — firmware failed to service watchdog | maintenance_only |
| 0xC0FF40 | NVM write failure — diagnostic session statistics not persisted | maintenance_only |
| 0xC0FE00 | Firmware image verification failed — CRC mismatch | check_at_next_halt |
| 0xC0FE10 | Firmware download incomplete — transfer interrupted | maintenance_only |
